quiz.title: Unit 11 Quiz 9, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced BrГёnsted-Lowry and Lewis Theories
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: BrГёnsted-Lowry Theory, Conjugate Acids and Bases, Acid-Base Strength Relationships, Amphoteric Behavior, Lewis Theory Introduction

questions.question: Which species is the strongest BrГёnstedвЂ“Lowry base?
questions.explanation: OHвЃ» (hydroxide ion) is the strongest BrГёnsted-Lowry base among these options. It readily accepts protons to form Hв‚‚O. HCl is an acid, Hв‚‚O is amphoteric but weak as a base, and NHв‚ѓ is a weaker base than OHвЃ».

questions.question: What remains unchanged during proton transfer?
questions.explanation: During proton transfer, the atoms involved remain unchanged вЂ” they are just rearranged. Protons move from one species to another, but no atoms are created or destroyed.

questions.question: The conjugate acid of HSвЃ» is:
questions.explanation: When HSвЃ» (hydrogen sulfide ion) accepts a proton (HвЃє), it becomes Hв‚‚S (hydrogen sulfide). Therefore, Hв‚‚S is the conjugate acid of HSвЃ».

questions.question: Which is a BrГёnsted-Lowry acid-base reaction in non-aqueous solvent?
questions.explanation: NHв‚„вЃє + NHв‚‚вЃ» в†’ 2NHв‚ѓ is a BrГёnsted-Lowry acid-base reaction. NHв‚„вЃє donates a proton (acting as an acid) to NHв‚‚вЃ» (acting as a base), forming two NHв‚ѓ molecules. This reaction can occur in non-aqueous solvents like liquid ammonia, demonstrating the broader applicability of BrГёnsted-Lowry theory beyond aqueous solutions.

questions.question: Which theory explains acidвЂ“base behavior based on electron pairs?
questions.explanation: Lewis theory defines acids as electron pair acceptors and bases as electron pair donors. This is the most comprehensive acid-base theory, explaining reactions that don't involve proton transfer.

questions.question: The conjugate acid of Hв‚‚O is:
questions.explanation: When Hв‚‚O accepts a proton (HвЃє), it becomes Hв‚ѓOвЃє (hydronium ion). Therefore, Hв‚ѓOвЃє is the conjugate acid of Hв‚‚O.

questions.question: The conjugate base of HCOв‚ѓвЃ» is:
questions.explanation: When HCOв‚ѓвЃ» donates a proton (HвЃє), it becomes COв‚ѓВІвЃ» (carbonate ion). Therefore, COв‚ѓВІвЃ» is the conjugate base of HCOв‚ѓвЃ».

questions.question: Which is a conjugate acid-base pair?
questions.explanation: NHв‚„вЃє and NHв‚ѓ differ by one proton (HвЃє). NHв‚„вЃє can donate a proton to become NHв‚ѓ, making them a conjugate acid-base pair.

questions.question: In the reaction Hв‚‚O + Hв‚‚O в‡Њ Hв‚ѓOвЃє + OHвЃ», water is acting as:
questions.explanation: This is the autoionization of water. One water molecule acts as an acid (donates a proton to become OHвЃ») while the other acts as a base (accepts a proton to become Hв‚ѓOвЃє). This shows water's amphoteric nature.

questions.question: The stronger the acid, the:
questions.explanation: There is an inverse relationship between acid strength and conjugate base strength. A strong acid has a weak conjugate base, and a weak acid has a strong conjugate base.

questions.question: Which is the strongest conjugate base?
questions.explanation: NHв‚‚вЃ» (amide ion) is the strongest base among these. It is the conjugate base of NHв‚ѓ (a very weak acid), and according to the inverse relationship, its conjugate base is very strong. OHвЃ» is also strong but less so than NHв‚‚вЃ».

questions.question: Which species is the conjugate base of Hв‚‚POв‚„вЃ»?
questions.explanation: When Hв‚‚POв‚„вЃ» (dihydrogen phosphate) donates a proton, it becomes HPOв‚„ВІвЃ» (hydrogen phosphate). Therefore, HPOв‚„ВІвЃ» is the conjugate base of Hв‚‚POв‚„вЃ».
